factors.summary: The company has identified a material weakness in internal controls over financial reporting, which could affect financial results and investor confidence.

evidence.text: We identified a material weakness in our internal control related to an ineffective information technology general control which, if not remediated appropriately or timely, could result in loss of investor confidence and adversely impact our stock price. Internal controls related to the operation of technology systems are critical to maintaining adequate internal control over financial reporting. [...] To the extent management is unable to remediate the identified issue timely, our ability to record, process and report financial information accurately, and to prepare financial statements within required time periods, could be adversely affected, which could subject us to litigation or investigations requiring management resources and payment of legal and other expenses, negatively affect investor confidence in our financial statements and adversely impact our stock price.
evidence.subsection: We are subject to taxation in multiple jurisdictions, and although we believe our tax estimates are reasonable, any adverse determinations in tax audits could negatively impact our financial results.

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating.

evidence.text: Volumes transacted in most services were down due to softening customer demand and from a slowdown in the global economy and retail customers' inventory build-up early in the year.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: Not mentioned.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting with North Asia being the largest export-oriented region.

evidence.text: North Asia is our largest export-oriented region and accounted for 34% of revenues, 39% of directly related cost of transportation and other expenses and 25% of operating income for the year ended December 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating.

evidence.text: Operating income and net earnings to shareholders both declined 4% due to higher operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: The end markets are experiencing a slowdown, particularly in North Asia and South Asia.

evidence.text: Tonnage decreased in almost all regions due to softening demand, pandemic related lockdowns in China and downtime caused by the cyber-attack with the largest decrease coming from exports out of North Asia, South Asia and North America.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are not explicitly mentioned as delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: No specific mention of measurable savings from cost actions.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Productivity is not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: Not mentioned.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing as available capacity exceeds demand.

evidence.text: As demand softened and pandemic restrictions subsided, port congestion cleared, availability of labor and equipment eased resulting in excess carrier capacity over demand.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Inventory levels are not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: Not mentioned.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: No notable incidents or recalls are mentioned regarding product quality or safety.

evidence.text: Not mentioned.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Management expects continued pressure on margins due to declining average buy and sell rates.

evidence.text: We expect that pricing volatility will continue as carriers adapt to lower demand, changing fuel prices and react to governmental trade policies and other regulations.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to expected challenges in maintaining margins and dealing with supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Management believes that the methods utilized in all of these areas are non-aggressive in approach and consistent in application, and that there are limited, if any, alternative accounting principles or methods which could be applied to these transactions.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Near-term risks include further declines in average buy and sell rates and increased costs due to inflation.

evidence.text: These conditions could result in further declines in average sell and buy rates in 2023. We also expect that pricing volatility will continue as carriers adapt to lower demand, changing fuel prices and react to governmental trade policies and other regulations.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is focused on maintaining its logistics services and expanding its geographic presence, but uncertainties remain due to global economic conditions.

evidence.text: We do not compete for overnight courier or small parcel business. As a non-asset-based carrier, we do not own or operate transportation assets.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, interest rates, and global economic conditions.

evidence.text: Fuel is an integral part of carriers' costs and impacts both our buy rates and sell rates, we would expect our revenues and costs to be impacted as carriers adjust rates for the effect of changing fuel prices.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: No specific near-term catalysts are mentioned that could meaningfully improve results.

evidence.text: Not mentioned.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

factors.summary: The global economy entered a recession due to the pandemic, which affected trade and demand for services, but rebounded in 2021 and continued into 2022. However, future unfavorable conditions could impact the business.

evidence.text: The global economy entered a recession as a result of the pandemic, which initially affected trade and negatively affected demand for our services for a period of time, before rebounding in 2021 and continuing into 2022. Future unfavorable economic conditions, rising interest rates and high inflation could result in lower freight volumes, reduced sell rates, higher operating expenses and may adversely affect Expeditors' revenues, operating results and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Global economic uncertainty impacted trade and could affect demand for our services or the financial stability of our service providers and customers.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, such as trade tensions and sanctions, could impact the company's operations and supply chain.

evidence.text: International trade is influenced by [...] changes and uncertainties in governmental policies and inter-governmental disputes, which could result in increased tariff rates, quota restrictions, trade barriers and other types of restrictions; [...] changes in the price and readily available quantities of oil and other petroleum-related products.
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations and sustainability requirements could increase costs and operational challenges, particularly with climate change initiatives and fuel efficiency standards.

evidence.text: The long-term effects of climate change are difficult to predict and may be widespread. [...] Government Regulation and Tax Risks
evidence.subsection: Climate change, including measures to address climate change, could adversely impact our business and financial results.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs and restrictions could impact the company's operations, particularly in international markets, leading to increased costs and reduced demand.

evidence.text: International trade is influenced by [...] changes and uncertainties in governmental policies and inter-governmental disputes, which could result in increased tariff rates, quota restrictions, trade barriers and other types of restrictions;
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges in retaining key employees due to the shift to remote work during the pandemic and potential turnover among remote workers.

evidence.text: The global pandemic caused disruptions to our work environment by requiring the majority of employees to work remotely during the height of the pandemic. As pandemic restrictions eased, we required employees to return to the office. As a result, for those individuals that prefer working remotely, we may experience a higher degree of turnover of key employees and lower employee satisfaction in the near future.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

factors.summary: The company relies on sophisticated technologies and is investing in system upgrades, but faces risks of disruptions and costs associated with maintaining and enhancing these systems.

evidence.text: Expeditors relies heavily and must compete based upon the flexibility and sophistication of the technologies utilized in performing our core businesses. [...] These efforts are inherently complex and if not managed properly could lead to disruptions in our operations or our ability to remain competitive.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

factors.summary: Rising interest rates could lead to higher operating expenses and reduced demand, potentially impacting the company's financial results.

evidence.text: Future unfavorable economic conditions, rising interest rates and high inflation could result in lower freight volumes, reduced sell rates, higher operating expenses and may adversely affect Expeditors' revenues, operating results and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Global economic uncertainty impacted trade and could affect demand for our services or the financial stability of our service providers and customers.

factors.summary: Currency exchange rate fluctuations and control regulations could impact the company's operations and financial results, particularly in international markets.

evidence.text: International trade is influenced by [...] currency exchange rates and currency control regulations;
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

factors.summary: The industry is highly competitive, with larger competitors having more resources and technological advantages, which could impact the company's ability to compete effectively.

evidence.text: The global logistics services industry is intensely competitive and is expected to remain so for the foreseeable future. [...] Many of these competitors have significantly more resources than Expeditors and may pursue acquisition opportunities and are developing new technologies to gain competitive advantages.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly competitive, and failure to compete or respond to customer requirements could damage our business and results of operations.

factors.summary: The company operates in a highly competitive market with diverse customer segments, but significant customers could pose concentration risks.

evidence.text: Many larger customers utilize the services of multiple logistics providers. [...] Increased competition and competitors' acceptance of expanded contractual terms coupled with customers’ dissatisfaction with elevated rates, scarce capacity, and extended transit times could result in loss of business, reduced revenues, reduced margins, higher operating costs or loss of market share, any of which would damage our results of operations, cash flows and financial condition.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly competitive, and failure to compete or respond to customer requirements could damage our business and results of operations.

factors.summary: The company has hired additional employees to manage supply chain challenges but faces potential workforce reductions and associated costs if business conditions change.

evidence.text: In the short term, any reductions in our workforce could result in significant additional expenses. Conversely, a failure to reduce compensation expense and other expenses in periods when the business environment does not support our workforce level will result in substantially lower compensation earned by the majority of employees. This may challenge our ability to retain and attract key employees to conduct our business successfully.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

factors.summary: The company is investing in system upgrades and technological improvements, which are critical for maintaining competitiveness but come with inherent risks of disruption.

evidence.text: We are continually enhancing our systems, including significant upgrades to core operating and accounting systems. These efforts are inherently complex and if not managed properly could lead to disruptions in our operations or our ability to remain competitive.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges in gaining profitable market share in a highly competitive environment, which could affect its ability to grow and compete.

evidence.text: Expeditors has historically relied primarily upon organic growth and has tended to avoid growth through acquisition. [...] To continue to grow organically, we must gain profitable market share in a highly competitive environment and successfully develop and market new service offerings.
evidence.subsection: Failure to grow and gain profitable market share could adversely impact our ability to remain competitive and could adversely impact our business.

factors.summary: The company is focused on developing new service offerings to meet evolving customer needs, but the success of these initiatives is uncertain.

evidence.text: Expeditors has historically relied primarily upon organic growth and has tended to avoid growth through acquisition. [...] To continue to grow organically, we must gain profitable market share in a highly competitive environment and successfully develop and market new service offerings.
evidence.subsection: Failure to grow and gain profitable market share could adversely impact our ability to remain competitive and could adversely impact our business.

factors.summary: The company has avoided acquisitions in the past, but future opportunities may require evaluating and integrating acquisitions, which could pose risks.

evidence.text: When investment opportunities arise, our success could be dependent on our ability to evaluate and integrate acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Failure to grow and gain profitable market share could adversely impact our ability to remain competitive and could adversely impact our business.

factors.summary: Pricing power is facing challenges as average buy and sell rates have declined due to improving transportation capacity and port congestion, and the inability to pass on sudden carrier rate increases to customers.

evidence.text: Average buy and sell rates, while still higher than historical levels, progressively declined throughout the year as imbalances between available capacity for transportation and demand and major port congestion have dissipated.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

evidence.text: Volatile market conditions can create situations where rate increases charged by carriers and other service providers are implemented with little or no advance notice. We often cannot pass these rate increases on to our customers in the same time frame, if at all. As a result, our yields and margins can be negatively impacted.
evidence.subsection: Difficulty in forecasting timing or volumes of customer shipments or rate changes by carriers could adversely impact our margins and operating results.

factors.summary: The company faces significant exposure to regulatory changes, particularly in tax laws and environmental and trade regulations, which could lead to increased tax liabilities, operational challenges, and financial volatility. The complex and rapidly changing regulatory environment increases the risk of non-compliance and imposes additional costs.

evidence.text: Current economic and political conditions make tax laws and regulations, or their interpretation and application, in any jurisdiction subject to significant change. Changes in tax laws or statutory tax rates, competing tax regimes, variability in the mix of pretax earnings we generate in the U.S, as compared to other countries, or new taxes in the United States or foreign jurisdictions could result in additional tax liabilities, or increased volatility in our effective tax rate and total tax expense.
evidence.subsection: We are subject to taxation in multiple jurisdictions, and although we believe our tax estimates are reasonable, any adverse determinations in tax audits could negatively impact our financial results.

evidence.text: Expeditors is affected by ever increasing regulations from a number of sources in the United States and in foreign locations in which we operate. [...] Furthermore, the implementation of these regulations, including deadlines and substantive requirements, can be driven by regulatory urgencies rather than industry's realistic ability to comply.
evidence.subsection: We are subject to a complex regulatory environment, and failure to comply with and adapt to these regulations could result in penalties or otherwise adversely impact our business.

factors.summary: The company faces significant risks related to litigation exposure, including potential material financial impacts from investigations and management time diversion. Additionally, a 2022 cyber-attack has increased vulnerability to future attacks, which could lead to legal claims and reputational damage.

evidence.text: As a multinational corporation, Expeditors is subject to formal or informal investigations from governmental authorities or others in the countries in which we do business. In addition, we may become subject to civil litigation with our customers, service providers and other parties with whom we do business. These investigations and litigation may require significant management time and could cause us to incur substantial additional legal and related costs, which may include fines, penalties or damages that could have a materially adverse impact on our financial results.
evidence.subsection: Investigations and litigation could require management time and or incur substantial legal costs or fines, penalties or damages, any of which could adversely impact on our financial results.

evidence.text: In February 2022, we were the subject of a targeted cyber-attack. [...] This cyber-attack, or any future cyber-attack could also result in increased vulnerability to attempts of fraud, legal claims and proceedings including potential breach of contract claims, reporting delays or errors; interference with regulatory reporting; an increase in costs to protect our systems and technology; or damage to our reputation.
evidence.subsection: Any significant disruptions to our network and systems continuity could have an adverse impact to our business and financial results.

factors.summary: Volume trends have been mixed, with a notable decline in unit volumes across most services and regions in recent years, particularly from 2022 compared to the strong volumes in 2021. However, there have been periods of recovery, such as the rebound in 2021 following the initial negative impacts of the pandemic, though current volumes remain subject to market demand and capacity constraints.

evidence.text: Volumes were lower in 2022 as a result of softening overall demand and compared to strong volumes in the same period in 2021 from customers converting to air shipments due to ocean port congestion.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

evidence.text: The global economy entered a recession as a result of the pandemic, which initially affected trade and negatively affected demand for our services for a period of time, before rebounding in 2021 and continuing into 2022. [...] When market demand significantly exceeds available capacity in a given market, which was the case for various services and markets at the beginning of the pandemic in 2020 and that continued through the first half of 2022, we may not always be able to find acceptable transportation or other service solutions to meet our customers’ needs or the routing and delivery of freight may be subject to delays that are outside of our control.
evidence.subsection: Global economic uncertainty impacted trade and could affect demand for our services or the financial stability of our service providers and customers.

factors.summary: Input costs have been negatively impacted by various factors, including rising fuel prices, port congestion, labor shortages, and supply chain disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. These issues have led to increased costs and have not been fully offset, posing risks to the company's financial performance.

evidence.text: Rising fuel prices, congestion at ports due to labor, truck and equipment shortages and disrupted sailing schedules resulted in continued high average buy rates in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic and various government reactions to it contributed to shortages of labor and capacity, and increased costs that continue to impact our operations. [...] Any significant disruption on the scale of the COVID-19 pandemic over an extended period could negatively affect our business and our financial results.
evidence.subsection: Risk Factors Overview

factors.summary: Labor costs are a significant expense that is subject to inflation and margin pressure due to shortages and increased expenses. The company faces challenges in retaining and recruiting employees, which could lead to higher compensation expenses and potential turnover, further impacting profitability.

evidence.text: Labor shortages and increased costs are contributing to operational challenges and higher expenses.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

evidence.text: In the long term, identifying, recruiting, hiring, training, and retaining employees is essential to our ability to operate and deliver our services, ability to grow and ultimately our future profitability. [...] We may incur higher compensation-related expense to recruit and retain and incur additional significant expense to hire third parties to perform tasks that have historically been performed by our employees.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure due to several factors, including higher operating expenses, challenges in passing on cost increases to customers, and operational inefficiencies from supply chain disruptions. These issues are contributing to a negative margin trajectory.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

evidence.text: Operating income and net earnings to shareholders both declined 4% due to higher operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: Difficulty in forecasting timing or volumes of customer shipments or rate changes by carriers could adversely impact our margins and operating results.

evidence.text: Volatile market conditions can create situations where rate increases charged by carriers and other service providers are implemented with little or no advance notice. We often cannot pass these rate increases on to our customers in the same time frame, if at all. As a result, our yields and margins can be negatively impacted.
evidence.subsection: Difficulty in forecasting timing or volumes of customer shipments or rate changes by carriers could adversely impact our margins and operating results.

factors.summary: The supply chain has shown improvement with the easing of port congestion and labor shortages, particularly by the fourth quarter. However, it remains vulnerable to potential future disruptions, as highlighted by the challenges faced during the pandemic and the need for a sustained number of employees.

evidence.text: Congestion at destination ports, shortages in equipment, labor and warehouse space that were significant at the beginning of the year cleared by the fourth quarter.
evidence.subsection: Introduction

evidence.text: The pandemic caused significant disruptions in global supply chain operations that were further exacerbated by congestion at destination ports and shortages of equipment, labor and warehouse space. [...] Though these disruptions substantially cleared by the fourth quarter of 2022, our number of employees at December 31, 2022, remain at historically high levels.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.
